content.problemStatement:
Chef and Divyam are playing a game with the following rules:


First, an integer X!X!X! is written on a board.
Chef and Divyam alternate turns; Chef plays first.
In each move, the current player should choose a positive integer DDD which is divisible by up to YYY distinct primes and does not exceed the integer currently written on the board. Note that 111 is not a prime.
DDD is then subtracted from the integer on the board, i.e. if the integer written on the board before this move was AAA, it is erased and replaced by A−DA-DA−D.
When one player writes 000 on the board, the game ends and this player wins.
Given XXX and YYY, determine the winner of the game.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains two space-separated integers XXX and YYY.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing the string `"Chef"` if Chef wins the game or `"Divyam"` if Divyam wins (without quotes).

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: Since D=1D = 1D=1 is divisible by 000 primes, Chef will write 000 and win the game in the first move.
Example case 2: Chef must choose DDD between 111 and 555 inclusive, since D=6D = 6D=6 is divisible by more than one prime. Then, Divyam can always choose 6−D6-D6−D, write 000 on the board and win the game.
